title: Tipsiness model: Buzz meter and handling degradation
description: Implement Buzz 0-100 meter with staged handling effects per the table in docs/GAME_DESIGN.md: steering drift, input lag, random wobble impulses, brief input mirroring, camera sway/blur shaders. Include slow decay and pickup-driven reduction. Signature mechanic.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in browser: diagonal-scroll riding with steering/speed, road-edge and cone collisions, crash/lives/finish states; BuzzSystem with staged drift, input lag, wobble, mirrored inputs, sway, vignette, mushy response. Dev keys B/N feed Buzz until beercycle-az9 lands.

title: Core riding: Paperboy-style diagonal scroll + steering
description: Constant forward auto-scroll on a pseudo-isometric diagonal road, left/right steering, speed up/slow down, collision detection with road-edge and obstacles, crash state. This is the core feel of the game and the base for the tipsiness model.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in browser: diagonal-scroll riding with steering/speed, road-edge and cone collisions, crash/lives/finish states; BuzzSystem with staged drift, input lag, wobble, mirrored inputs, sway, vignette, mushy response. Dev keys B/N feed Buzz until beercycle-az9 lands.

title: Scaffold project: Phaser 3 + TypeScript + Vite
description: Initialize the web game project per docs/GAME_DESIGN.md tech stack: Vite + TS + Phaser 3, basic scene structure (Boot, Menu, Game, Results), dev server, static build. Everything else builds on this.
close_reason: Vite+TS+Phaser scaffold complete: Boot/Menu/Game/Results scenes, placeholder ride, LeaderboardStore over localStorage, strict tsc build passing, full loop verified in browser. Build docs added to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.

title: Finalize game design decisions
description: Resolve open questions in docs/GAME_DESIGN.md: real vs parody brewery names, leaderboard backend or static-only, mobile controls in MVP. Blocks nothing technically but should be settled before art/level content.
close_reason: Decided: parody brewery names, localStorage leaderboard behind a LeaderboardStore interface (backend swappable later), keyboard-only controls for MVP. Recorded in docs/GAME_DESIGN.md

title: Player characters: add AAFRAN
description:
Sixth playable rider: AAFRAN — ivory tower professor who takes no shit from her students.

Visual reference (from photo): straight brown chin-length bob, gray-blue eyes, skeptical arched brow, subtle closed-mouth smirk, gold nose stud (left), star tattoo on right shoulder, black tank top, thin gold necklace, gray bag strap.

Scope: 8-state portrait, bike sprite variant, voice lines, trait card in rider select.
close_reason: AAFRAN added as 6th rider: 8-state portrait (ash-brown bob, raised brow, heavy-lidded gray-blue stare, flat mouth, nose stud, black tank, star tattoo, gold necklace), bike variant, stern-professor voice lines. Verified menu/ride/LAST CALL in headless Chromium.

title: Player characters: add PEN
description:
Fifth playable rider: PEN — smooth jazz clarinet player, laid-back vibe.

Visual reference (from photo): shoulder-length brown hair half pulled back, big warm smile, no glasses, oversized light gray/lavender tee, tan crossbody strap.

Scope: 8-state portrait, bike sprite variant, voice lines, trait card in rider select.
close_reason: PEN added as 5th rider: 8-state portrait (brown half-up hair, open/chill eyes, big grin, lavender tee, crossbody strap), bike variant with strap detail, smooth-jazz voice lines. Verified menu/ride/LAST CALL in headless Chromium.

title: Player characters: add JillBake
description:
Start supporting multiple playable characters. First new character: JillBake — a Berkeley feminist fighting the patriarchy one student at a time.

Visual reference (from photo): long straight blue hair, red/maroon frame sunglasses with mirrored lenses, pink/magenta hoodie, nose studs on both sides, smiling.

Scope:
- Character select step (Menu scene or pre-run) with the existing default rider plus JillBake.
- Pixel-art sprite set for JillBake at the game's 480x270 internal res style (keep pixelArt: true, no sub-pixel sizing).
- Character identity shown in HUD/Results (initials default, flavor text).
- Structure character data so more characters can be added later (name, sprite key, tagline, maybe stat tweaks).
close_reason: JillBake added as 4th rider: 8-state portrait (blue hair, red mirrored shades, nose studs, pink hoodie), bike sprite variant, voice lines, trait card. Menu/Game/Results/GameOver flows verified in headless Chromium.

title: Per-brewery interior backdrops during stops
description: When a stop opens, replace the road/grass gameplay backdrop with a full-screen pixel-art interior/branding scene for that brewery, inspired by the real parody source. Research notes (2026-07-19, web-verified): Mountain Sun Pub = hippie living-room vibe, walls covered in eclectic art and old Grateful Dead/String Cheese concert posters, tapestries, kooky creatures hanging from the ceiling, western/Native-pioneer motifs. Upslope = 'apres everything' outdoorsy can-first brand, industrial Flatiron Park taproom with garage doors, cans styled dirty from snow/river/trail adventures, teal/tan palette. Wild Woods = literal camping theme: beetle-kill pine walls (blue-streaked), tree-cross-section bar, warehouse ceiling, smoky campfire red + S'mores porter. From established knowledge: West Flanders = Belgian brasserie on Pearl (dark wood, chalices, abbey vibe); Boulder Beer = Colorado's first craft brewery (1979), copper kettles, Buffalo Gold legacy taproom; Sanitas = east-Boulder patio life, dog-friendly, resident taco truck, Flatirons view; Avery = cathedral-scale Gunbarrel facility, stainless tanks, stacked bourbon barrel room, White Rascal devil-mascot Belgian program; Twisted Pine = experimental casual taproom, ghost-pepper chili beer infamy; Fate = sleek modern gastro-brewpub, closed 2018 (in-game: ghostly/memorial treatment fits the 'gone but still pouring' tagline).
design: Art direction per venue (procedural pixel-art, drawn like the existing canvas backdrops): MOUNTAIN MOON: warm wood-brown room, tie-dye tapestry rectangles, tiny framed psychedelic posters, string lights, a dangling googly creature. BEST FLANDERS: dark wood paneling, stained-glass window shapes in ruby/amber, chalice shelf. BOLDER BEER CO: copper kettle domes, 'SINCE 1979' pennant, gold neon. UPSLIP: corrugated wall + open garage door showing mountains, wall of teal cans, hanging kayak/bike. SANITASTIC: outdoor patio at dusk - string lights, taco-truck silhouette, dogs, Flatirons behind. AVIARY: towering stacked barrel wall, catwalk, purple gloom, one red-eyed bird. TWISTY SPRUCE: chalkboard menu wall with WHAT?! scrawls, chili pepper garlands, fire extinguisher. MILD WOODS: blue-streaked pine planks, tree-slice tables, glowing pixel campfire, mounted antlers. FETE: elegant gray room, dust sheets over furniture, one lit tap handle, faint halo glow. Implementation: add id slug to Brewery; textures bg_{id} at 480x270 drawn in pixelart.ts; GameScene shows a scrollFactor-0 image at depth ~998 (under vignette/panel) on enterStop, hides on exitStop; world objects can stay (covered). Keep each under ~40 lines of ctx drawing; share helpers (plank wall, string lights, framed poster).
close_reason: Nine researched procedural interiors shipped and verified in browser (Mountain Moon tapestry den, Best Flanders stained glass, Aviary barrel wall confirmed visually; all nine generated). Unblocks beercycle-zgh.

title: Brewery mini-game diversity and flow improvements
description: All timing-style beers share one oscillating-marker interaction; stops can also be farmed indefinitely by re-ordering. Add mini-game variety and tighten stop flow, including a cap on pour/round attempts per stop.
design: Flow: cap rounds per stop (e.g., 'your tab' = 3 beers, shown as pint icons that empty; bartender cuts you off with a vocab line when spent); auto-return to the tap list after the result flash instead of requiring an extra press; fix ENTER dropped during the 0.9s result window (also noted on beercycle-mz8). New mechanic candidates (2-3 is enough): FLIGHT for drellis-coded breweries — order-matching taste test across 3 small glasses (LEFT/RIGHT/tap sequence memory); DARTS at dive-ier stops — two-axis oscillating crosshair, tap to throw; COASTER FLIP — double-tap rhythm (flip then catch); KEEP THE HEAD — after pouring, briefly balance a wobbling meter (Buzz-influenced) before drinking. Map mechanics per-brewery in the Brewery def (like taps) so venues feel distinct; stouts' fill pour could allow exactly one re-pour attempt on overflow ('foam mulligan').
close_reason: Shipped: darts + flight house games mapped per-brewery (6 venues), 3-beer tab with vocab cutoff, buffered result inputs, idle auto-return, foam mulligan. Browser-verified: darts 3-throw loop +130 with auto-return and one-shot button; flight [0,0,1,2] tap-repeat +480 exact; cutoff after 3 beers; mulligan once then foam scoring +176 exact.

title: Level variety: brewery previews in transitions, road shape changes
description: Routes currently differ only in palette, length, and hazard counts. Add real between-level variety: a visually interesting preview of the NEXT route's breweries on the transition screen, and road geometry variation along a route if feasible.
design: Transition preview: on the interim Bugle (or a dedicated card), show the next route's marquee — brewery glyphs + names + signature tap (glass sprite + style), plus a hazard forecast line ('expect: geese, loading zones'). Road geometry: ROAD_WIDTH is currently a constant; move it into RouteDef (wide East Boulder, narrow Creek-Path-style squeeze) — projection already derives from lat ranges so this is mostly plumbing collision/spawn bounds. Curves: cheap Paperboy-style approach is a curving centerline — roadCenterLat(d) function per route segment that drifts the road's lateral center over distance, forcing active steering without touching the FORWARD projection; sharper segments at higher route index. Per-segment set dressing (creek strip, industrial props) pairs with the width changes.
close_reason: Shipped: per-route road width + sine centerline with segmented road rendering, all world objects centerline-relative (spawns, collisions, triggers, bounds); Bugle transition marquee with brewery glyphs/glasses/names + hazard forecast. Verified in browser: THE HILL renders visibly curved and narrow with carried state; transition page shows Upslip/Sanitastic marquee and forecast.

title: Differentiate Game Over from level transitions
description: Right now the run-ending Bugle and the between-routes Bugle are nearly identical cream newspaper pages — players can't tell at a glance whether the run is over or continuing. Keep the newspaper for LEVEL TRANSITIONS (reading the paper between routes is the Paperboy homage) and give GAME OVER a visually distinct, comedic, terminal screen.
design: Game Over concepts (pick during implementation): black/red palette so it can't be confused with the cream interim page; pixel vignette of the crashed bike with a spinning front wheel and circling stars; variant art per ending — BUI bust shows the cop writing a ticket, crash-out shows the tumbled bike, with the rider's vocab quote as an epitaph. Comedic 'LAST CALL' banner in big type; deflating-tire or sad-trombone chiptune sting (audio.ts). The high-score list and final tally can live below the graphic; newspaper stays interim-only. Transition page could also get a clearer CONTINUE banner to reinforce the difference from the other side.
close_reason: LAST CALL GameOverScene shipped: black/red palette, flickering neon, crash tableau (toppled bike + dead portrait + orbiting stars) and BUI tableau (cop + citation #420), vocab epitaph, sad-trombone sting, leaderboard submit moved here for failures; Bugle reserved for transitions and victory. Both variants verified in browser incl. red high-score entry.

title: Tap selection + style-specific chug mechanics
description: Chug flow gains a PICK phase: LEFT/RIGHT selects a tap (glass sprite + name + style/ABV readout), SPACE orders. Mechanics per style: parameterized timing marker (fast IPA, easy lager), JITTER for sours (random marker kicks), HOLD-AND-RELEASE fill pour for stouts/porters (overfill = foam). After-effects while riding: heavy legs (stout), refreshed floor (lager), chili rush (red overlay + forced speed + shake). Escalation per round retained.
close_reason: Shipped in e4b41bc: beers.ts data model with ABV/style formulas and 20 parody beers across 9 breweries; glassware + glyph textures and accent-themed chug panel; tap PICK phase + timing/jitter/fill mechanics + heavy/refresh/chili riding effects; vocab.ts rider lines wired into panel, crash popups, and Bugle quotes. Verified in browser: Mountain Moon tap list w/ DRELLIS enter line, FM PILS timing lock, PUCK'S PORTER overfill FOAM + heavy-legs timer + correct Buzz math, Bugle DRELLIS quote.

Wire into chug panel (enter line, perfect feedback), crash popups, and pass a quote + rider name to ResultsScene so the Bugle subhead quotes the rider (bust quote when cited, finish quote when home, crash quote on mishap).
close_reason: Shipped in e4b41bc: beers.ts data model with ABV/style formulas and 20 parody beers across 9 breweries; glassware + glyph textures and accent-themed chug panel; tap PICK phase + timing/jitter/fill mechanics + heavy/refresh/chili riding effects; vocab.ts rider lines wired into panel, crash popups, and Bugle quotes. Verified in browser: Mountain Moon tap list w/ DRELLIS enter line, FM PILS timing lock, PUCK'S PORTER overfill FOAM + heavy-legs timer + correct Buzz math, Bugle DRELLIS quote.

Chug panel restyled per brewery: accent border, glyph next to name, tagline line.
close_reason: Shipped in e4b41bc: beers.ts data model with ABV/style formulas and 20 parody beers across 9 breweries; glassware + glyph textures and accent-themed chug panel; tap PICK phase + timing/jitter/fill mechanics + heavy/refresh/chili riding effects; vocab.ts rider lines wired into panel, crash popups, and Bugle quotes. title: Beer data model: styles, ABV effects, per-brewery tap lists
description: New src/systems/beers.ts: BeerDef (id/name/style/abv/color/head/glass shape) + derivations mechanicFor(style), effectFor(style), buzzFor/pointsFor from ABV, marker speed per style. Extend Brewery (src/systems/breweries.ts) with accent color, glyph id, tagline, taps[]. Populate all 9 breweries in src/systems/routes.ts with 2-3 parody beers each per the epic design (beercycle-bmi).
close_reason: Shipped in e4b41bc: beers.ts data model with ABV/style formulas and 20 parody beers across 9 breweries; glassware + glyph textures and accent-themed chug panel; tap PICK phase + timing/jitter/fill mechanics + heavy/refresh/chili riding effects; vocab.ts rider lines wired into panel, crash popups, and Bugle quotes. Verified in browser: Mountain Moon tap list w/ DRELLIS enter line, FM PILS timing lock, PUCK'S PORTER overfill FOAM + heavy-legs timer + correct Buzz math, Bugle DRELLIS quote.

title: Brewery depth: tap lists, signage, style mechanics, rider voice
description: Make brewery stops the heart of the game. Each parody brewery gets a themed identity (accent color, tiny logo glyph, tagline) and a 2-3 beer tap list where the beer STYLE is a gameplay choice: ABV drives Buzz and points, and style picks the chug mechanic and an after-effect on riding. Player picks a tap with LEFT/RIGHT before each round. Also: per-rider voice lines replace generic strings at key events, and the Bugle quotes the rider.
design: Style->mechanic: sours JITTER the timing marker (random phase kicks, 'the pucker'); stouts/porters use a HOLD-AND-RELEASE pour (hold SPACE, release near the fill line; overfill = foam everywhere, bad score); IPAs/DIPAs run a fast marker; lagers/pils/kolsch run slow (easy) markers. Style->after-effect while riding: stout/porter = heavy legs (speed capped ~5s); lager/pils/kolsch = refreshed (speed floor raised ~5s); chili beer (GHOST FACE CHILLAH at Twisty Spruce) = red screen flash + forced near-max speed 4s + camera shake. ABV formulas: buzz=round(abv*2.4) so the 13.5% barrel-aged stout is a genuine decision; points=round(60+abv*12). Glassware pixel art per style: shaker pint, nonic, tulip (sours/wits), snifter (quad/BA stout), CAN for Upslip (aluminum is a lifestyle). Brewery identities: Mountain Moon (moon glyph, 'cash only. it's a vibe.'), Best Flanders (diamond, 'ah, belgium.', sour house), Bolder Beer Co (sun, 'bolder since 1979.', BUFF GOLD lager), Upslip (mountain, cans), Sanitastic (flower, 'dog friendly. extremely.'), Aviary (bird, 'barrels all the way down.', RAVEN'S RESERVE 13.5%), Twisty Spruce (pine, 'we put WHAT in it?'), Mild Woods (campfire, S'MORES STOUT), Fete (halo, 'gone but still pouring.', RIP joke). Vocab: per-rider line tables for enter-stop/perfect-pour/crash/bust/finish; Bugle subhead quotes the rider by name.
close_reason: Epic complete; all children shipped and verified.

title: Doom-style reactive HUD portrait
description: GameScene HUD corner portrait of the chosen avatar that reacts to game state per the design notes in beercycle-uju: Buzz-staged degradation, crash wince, cop-sight sweat, chug party face, X-eyes at run end, smug on perfect pour. Slight rotation sway synced to the BuzzSystem camera sway so the face rocks when drunk. Keep it out of the road's visual path (bottom-left is emptiest during play).
close_reason: All four shipped in commit 2d22159: 24x24 code-drawn portraits x8 states per rider, menu character select + polaroid trio splash, reactive Doom-style HUD portrait with Buzz rocking, per-avatar bike sprite variants incl. helmetless HOSKINS. Verified select cycling, portrait state transitions (hammered->sober, sweat), and variant textures in browser.

title: Pixel portrait set: 3 avatars x expression states
description: Author ~24x24 pixel-map portraits in src/art/pixelart.ts for each of the three riders (canonical visual descriptions in parent epic beercycle-uju). Expression states per rider: sober, tipsy (flushed), hammered (swirly eyes), crash-wince, sweat (cop nearby), chug/party, X-eyes (run over), smug (perfect pour). Share a state-suffix texture key scheme like avatar1_sober so the HUD can swap by string. Distinctive pixels that must read at size: R1 green-mirror shades + silver beard + hi-vis yellow; R2 wire glasses + open mouth + plaid + NO helmet (sandy hair); R3 dark shades + goatee + gray shirt + backpack straps.
close_reason: All four shipped in commit 2d22159: 24x24 code-drawn portraits x8 states per rider, menu character select + polaroid trio splash, reactive Doom-style HUD portrait with Buzz rocking, per-avatar bike sprite variants incl. helmetless HOSKINS. Verified select cycling, portrait state transitions (hammered->sober, sweat), and variant textures in browser.

Splash art: pixel recreation of the trio selfie per the design notes in beercycle-uju (R1 foreground grin, other two photobombing, dusk Flatirons behind). Persist last-chosen avatar in localStorage. Ask user for real nicknames before shipping names.
close_reason: All four shipped in commit 2d22159: 24x24 code-drawn portraits x8 states per rider, menu character select + polaroid trio splash, reactive Doom-style HUD portrait with Buzz rocking, per-avatar bike sprite variants incl. helmetless HOSKINS. Verified select cycling, portrait state transitions (hammered->sober, sweat), and variant textures in browser.

title: Player avatars: the three jokers
description:
Add human personality via three selectable player avatars based on the user's cycling friends (photo provided in session 2026-07-19; visual descriptions below are the canonical reference since the photo is not stored).

RIDER 1 'the selfie arm' (left of photo): white/silver vented bike helmet, white-frame wraparound sunglasses with green-teal mirrored lenses, full silver-gray beard, huge open-mouth grin, bright chartreuse/hi-vis yellow jersey. Radiates stoke.

RIDER 2 'the photobomber' (middle): NO helmet, thinning sandy-blond hair slightly windblown, rectangular wire-rim glasses, gray-brown full beard, mouth wide open in exaggerated mock-shock/excitement, blue-and-white plaid button-up shirt. Chaos energy.

RIDER 3 'the skeptic' (right): white helmet with olive-green chin strap, dark rimless sunglasses, tidy salt-and-pepper goatee-beard, closed-mouth wry half-smile, gray t-shirt with gray backpack straps. Deadpan calm.

Names are placeholders — ask the user what the guys go by in-game.

Note: these are real-people likenesses in a public repo and public deployed game; user initiated this, but confirm the friends are cool with it before wide sharing.
design:
Core idea: a Doom-style reactive status portrait. The chosen avatar's pixel face lives in a corner of the HUD and degrades with the Buzz meter: sober smile -> flushed grin -> half-lidded sway (portrait rocks a few degrees) -> swirly/crossed eyes at 80+. Event reactions: wince/grimace + stars during crash tumble, sweat drops while a cop is in sight (suspicion > 0), party face during chug minigame, X-eyes on run-ending mishap, smug face on PERFECT POUR. Portrait size ~24x24 at 480x270 internal res, drawn via the existing pixel-map pipeline in src/art/pixelart.ts.

Character select: menu LEFT/RIGHT cycles the three portraits with name below; SPACE confirms. Rider road sprite swaps jersey/helmet palette to match (yellow jersey+helmet / plaid blue NO helmet (hair pixels!) / gray+helmet+backpack straps). Rider 2 riding helmetless is itself a joke opportunity (Boulder would absolutely comment).

Splash screen: recreate the selfie as pixel art — Rider 1 huge in the left foreground grinning with an outstretched selfie arm hinted at the frame edge, the other two photobombing from the right, all in front of the dusk Flatirons. Could replace or overlay the current menu art.

Bugle integration: headlines could name the chosen rider's nickname; high scores default initials to the avatar's initials.
notes: Names locked by user (photo left-to-right): 'dwnwrd' = R1 selfie-arm (hacker, VEGAN — flavor: his taco pickup reads TOFU TACO); 'hoskins' = R2 helmetless photobomber (rotund — draw wider face/fuller cheeks; prankster — near-miss popup can smirk); 'drellis' = R3 skeptic (professorial, slim, BEER SNOB — perfect-pour feedback reads like tasting notes). Initials for high scores: DWN / HOS / DRE.
close_reason: Epic complete: names DWNWRD/HOSKINS/DRELLIS locked, all child tasks shipped and verified. Reminder to user: confirm the friends are OK with likenesses in the public game before sharing wide.

title: Touch controls for mobile
description: Post-MVP per locked decisions in docs/GAME_DESIGN.md: tilt or touch-zone steering, tap for chug minigame. Keyboard-only was the MVP scope.
design: Unified pointer input rather than a separate mobile mode (mouse benefits too). Riding: touching the screen steers toward the touch (left/right of the bike anchor with a deadzone) — raw input still flows through the Buzz pipeline (lag/mirror/drift) so tipsiness feels identical on touch; +/- speed buttons bottom-right, second-finger friendly (activePointers=3). Chug panel: tap a tap-slot to select, tap again to order, tap panel to slam (timing), hold anywhere to pour (fill), [ONE MORE]/[RIDE ON] tap buttons in result phase. Menus: tap arrows to cycle rider, tap anywhere to start/advance. Viewport: user-scalable=no + touch-action none to kill scroll/zoom gestures. No ESC or pause equivalent on touch yet — noted as follow-up.
close_reason: Unified pointer input shipped in 9132443: touch-steer toward finger through the Buzz pipeline, on-screen +/- speed buttons (3 active pointers), tap-select/tap-order/tap-slam/hold-pour in the chug panel with ONE MORE / RIDE ON buttons, tap-to-advance menus and Bugle, zoom/scroll gestures suppressed. Verified in an 812x375 pane with real pointer events: rider select taps, tap-to-start, touch steering (steered clear off the road and crashed - it works), tap-order LUNAR HAZE with CLEAN COMMIT perfect, RIDE ON exit, Bugle tap-advance. Follow-up noted: no pause/bail equivalent on touch.

title: Difficulty and balance tuning pass
description: Playtest-driven tuning: NPC density near spawn feels punishing (crashed out in seconds on Pearl St during testing), BUI suspicion window may be too strict to ever trigger organically, Buzz decay vs drink amounts, cone spacing, near-miss radius. Play each route start-to-finish and tune constants in GameScene/routes.ts/buzz.ts.
notes: Additional tuning items found 2026-07-19: (1) ENTER pressed during the 0.9s chug result flash is silently dropped — buffer it or shorten the lockout. (2) Fill-pour rate 0.85/s and sweet zone 0.8-0.97 need feel testing on a real keyboard. (3) Chili forced-speed 175 vs cone density on EAST BOULDER may be brutal — intended, but verify it's fun-brutal.

Verified in browser: both stops triggered, +88 (x1) and +160/+230 (x2 streak) scored, Buzz meter filled.

title: Touch: pause and bail-out controls
description: Touch players have no ESC (bail to results) or P (pause) equivalent. Add a small pause tap-target in the HUD that opens a paused overlay with RESUME / BAIL OUT options; keyboard keys keep working. Filed from beercycle-bkg.

title: Per-avatar rider road sprite variants
description: Swap the on-road rider sprite palette/pixels by chosen avatar: R1 yellow jersey + white helmet, R2 plaid blue shirt + bare sandy hair (no helmet), R3 gray shirt + helmet + backpack straps. Two pedal frames each, same texture-key swap pattern as bike_a/bike_b in src/art/pixelart.ts.
close_reason: All four shipped in commit 2d22159: 24x24 code-drawn portraits x8 states per rider, menu character select + polaroid trio splash, reactive Doom-style HUD portrait with Buzz rocking, per-avatar bike sprite variants incl. helmetless HOSKINS. Verified select cycling, portrait state transitions (hammered->sober, sweat), and variant textures in browser.

title: High-score initials entry
description: Results screen currently records every score as CYC. Add a 3-letter arcade-style initials entry on the final Bugle page when the score makes the top 10.

title: Flagstaff climb bonus stage
description: Paperboy training-course analog from docs/GAME_DESIGN.md: a pure handling-test bonus route at current Buzz after East Boulder, big points, no breweries. Needs a climb/speed twist (fighting gravity) to feel distinct.

title: Deploy: static hosting for MVP
description: Static build pipeline and hosting (GitHub Pages or Netlify), plus a playable-build checklist. No backend for MVP.
close_reason: Repo made public per user; GitHub Pages enabled with workflow source; Actions workflow builds Vite and deploys dist/ on push to main. Live and verified at https://guifreelife.com/beercycle/ (account-level custom domain applies to project pages).

title: Audio: chiptune soundtrack and SFX
description: One chiptune track for MVP (Pearl Street), SFX set (bike bell, bottle clink, crash, crowd), and Buzz-linked music detune/warble effect.
close_reason: Runtime WebAudio chiptune: bass+lead loop, full SFX set, Buzz-linked detune warble, mute toggle, autoplay-safe lazy init. Verified context running and sequencer scheduling in browser.
